checks.finding: The T-004/T-007 primary citations verify exactly: reports/implementation/evidence-deep-verify/T-004.md:79-86 (BLOCKED/190-of-191 snapshot text), reports/quality-gate/2026-07-09T020500Z-evidence-deep-verify-T-004.md:41 (Minor/Accepted finding, matching quoted text), reports/implementation/evidence-deep-verify/T-007.md:80,166 (flat path + '197, pass 197'), and reports/quality-gate/2026-07-09T021500Z-evidence-deep-verify-T-007.md:40 (matching quoted text) -- all confirmed. However, the frequency/history bullet misattributes its sdd-domain citation: 'sdd-domain's recorded it inside its broader FP-05 (5 tasks)'. sdd-domain's actual FP-05 ('Gate-tooling contract drift', reports/retrospective/2026-07-07T0633Z-sdd-domain.md:166) has Frequency '4 independent gaps... 2 of them observed as live blockers this period' and covers Task-ID-line/Feature-line/placeholder-waiver/round>1 format gaps (the WFI-005 theme), not stale-narrative drift. The '5 tasks' stale-evidence finding actually lives at sdd-domain's FP-02 ('Stale evidence in implementation reports (hashes/prose drift after fixes)', reports/retrospective/2026-07-07T0633Z-sdd-domain.md:107-119, 'Frequency: 6 occurrences across 5 tasks'). WFI-006 cites the wrong FP number for this data point (this same mislabeling also appears verbatim in the source retrospective's FP-01 paragraph at reports/retrospective/2026-07-09T0245Z-evidence-deep-verify.md:47, but the WFI carries the error forward uncorrected into its own Problem Evidence and Expected Effect/Verification Metric baseline text).

No issues found. The hypothesis names a specific mechanism: neither the report template nor the quality verification gate's process instructions state that a report's numbers/statuses/paths are commit-scoped and may be superseded by a later same-feature event (another task's edit to the same surface, or a gate-phase artifact normalization); nothing in the process reconciles the frozen report against gate-time reality, so the mismatch is only caught when a reviewer happens to notice it -- this explains recurrence across models/risk tiers rather than restating the symptom ('reports go stale').

No issues found. Root Cause Hypothesis, the Proposed Change Description column, and Expected Effect use only generic terms ('quality verification gate', 'implementation phase', 'gate process', 'independent gate reviewer'). 'sdd-quality-loop' and 'quality-gate' appear only inside the Target File column as literal repository paths (plugins/sdd-quality-loop/skills/quality-gate/SKILL.md), matching the WFI-005 precedent that path citations in the Target File column are not prose usage and are not flagged.

Both Proposed Change rows name specific files (not directories) with non-vague descriptions, but both Target Files sit inside plugins/, which is a Major finding regardless of description: Row 1 -- `plugins/sdd-implementation/templates/implementation-report.template.md` (Add a 'Snapshot Notice' line...); Row 2 -- `plugins/sdd-quality-loop/skills/quality-gate/SKILL.md` (Add one instruction to the gate process...). Confirmed applied via `git diff origin/main` (7 lines added to the template, 6 lines added to SKILL.md step 9), so the description is concrete and truthful -- the finding is scope, not vagueness.

No issues found. Names a specific metric (stale-narrative-vs-current-state Minor findings per feature, recorded in quality verification gate reports) and a quantitative target (baseline 2 this period, decreasing to at most 1 in the next completed feature), plus an explicit non-decreasing-guard statement (no check/gate/threshold removed).

No issues found. Exactly one primary Target-Metric is named, with Baseline (2, evidence-deep-verify, 2026-07-09 -- prior periods listed only as disclosed historical context, not competing primary baselines), Target (<=1), and Horizon/checkpoint (next completed feature retrospective) all present.

No issues found. The plan names the exact finding-text pattern to scan for in every quality verification gate report next cycle, cites the two specific gate report rows that anchor the current-period count (2026-07-09T020500Z-evidence-deep-verify-T-004.md:41 and 2026-07-09T021500Z-evidence-deep-verify-T-007.md:40), states a pass/fail threshold (<=1), and adds a secondary specific check (Snapshot Notice line present from first commit; no reviewer edits to a frozen implementation report).

Both Proposed Change rows target files inside plugins/: Row 1 -- `plugins/sdd-implementation/templates/implementation-report.template.md`; Row 2 -- `plugins/sdd-quality-loop/skills/quality-gate/SKILL.md`. Per wfi-category-guide.md Section 1, a plugin-improvement WFI's application mechanism is a GitHub Issue created after audit completion, not a direct Proposed Change table edit to plugins/ files. This is reinforced by the WFI's own source material: the current retrospective's own Proposed Improvements table recommends the target as 'AGENTS.md / implementation-report checklist (project-side; require a final prose/count/path refresh pass immediately before gate submission)' (reports/retrospective/2026-07-09T0245Z-evidence-deep-verify.md:70), and the earlier ci-mcp retrospective made the identical project-side recommendation (reports/retrospective/2026-07-08T033000Z.md:80) -- both point to a project-side AGENTS.md target, not the two plugins/ files this WFI actually proposes.

proposed_revisions.change: In the 'Frequency and history' bullet, change the sdd-domain citation from "sdd-domain's recorded it inside its broader FP-05 (5 tasks)" to "sdd-domain's recorded it as FP-02 ('Stale evidence in implementation reports', 6 occurrences across 5 tasks: T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008, T-011)", citing reports/retrospective/2026-07-07T0633Z-sdd-domain.md:107-119 instead of the FP-05 section.
proposed_revisions.rationale: Resolves the EVIDENCE-CITED Major finding: the cited FP number does not match the row that actually contains the '5 tasks' stale-evidence data; FP-05 is a different friction class (format/contract-drift gaps already addressed by WFI-005).

proposed_revisions.change: Replace both plugins/ rows with a single row whose Target File is the GitHub Issue that wfi-audit-cycle creates per wfi-category-guide.md Section 4 (carrying the same Snapshot Notice and gate-process-instruction content into the Issue body verbatim). If a project-side companion is warranted, add a separate row targeting AGENTS.md (the target the source retrospective itself recommends, reports/retrospective/2026-07-09T0245Z-evidence-deep-verify.md:70) with a concrete project-root change, e.g. an implementation-report checklist reminder to record current values before gate submission.
proposed_revisions.rationale: Resolves both CHANGE-CONCRETE and NO-PLUGIN-SCOPE-CREEP Major findings: plugins/ paths are out of scope for a WFI's direct Proposed Change table regardless of description quality; the category guide's own routing mechanism for plugin-improvement WFIs is a GitHub Issue, and the retrospective sources this WFI draws from already recommended a project-side AGENTS.md target.
